solutions.solution:
(c) Method I
Benches Roads Benches Woods
Desks Chairs Desks Chairs
Benches Chairs
Desks Roads
I. Some roads are chairs. (✓)
II. No chair is a desk. (✓)
Method II
All desks are benches. (A-type)
No bench is a chair. (E-type)
All chairs are roads. (A-type)
From Statement 3,
All chairs are roads → Conversion → Some roads are chairs
= Conclusion I
From Statements 1 and 2,
A + E = E-type conclusion
No desk is a chair → Conversion → No chair is desk
= Conclusion II
Hence, both Conclusions I and II follow.

(d) Jungles Pens Boys Needles
I. Some needles are pens. (✗)
II. Some boys are pens. (✗)

(e) Stones Tents Stones Walls
Walls Tents
I. Some walls are stones. (✓)
II. Some walls are tents. (✓)

(b) Tables Chairs Woods Tables
Woods Stones Stones
Chairs
I. No stone is table. (✗)
II. Some stones are woods (✓)

(c) Fruits Red Fruits Red
Mangoes Vegetables Mangoes
I. No fruit is red. (✗)
II. Some fruits are red. (✗)
Complementary pair (E-I)

(b) Enterances Doors Gate Windows
I. Atleast some windows are gates. (✗)
II. No gate is an entrance. (✓)

(b) Green Blue Letters Black
I. No letter is green. (✗)
II. Most blue are blacks. (✓)

(d) Method I
Pens Buttons Books Pencils
I. Some buttons are pens. (✗)
II. Some pencils are books. (✗)
Method II
Some books are pens. (I-type)
Some pens are pencils. (I-type)
Some pencils are buttons. (I-type)
From Statements 2 and 3,
I + I = Not a valid statement
